chunks.text: Freedom of expression is one of the essential pillars of democracy.
But how far should that freedom extend?
This is one of the most controversial questions in modern society.
Hate speech, discriminatory expression, and deliberate misinformation.
Should these fall within the range of free speech?
Many countries have placed limitation on forms of expression that cause harm.
On the other hand, regulation carries the danger of excess.
If a government controls expression too much, citizens become afraid and stop raising their voices.
Finding the balance between freedom and safety is extremely difficult.
The emergence of the internet has made this issue considerably more complex.
A statement posted on social media spreads instantly, and removing it is virtually impossible.
Activist continue to fight to protect freedom of expression.
there is a growing demand for laws that protect the victim of harmful speech.
What matters most is the attitude of listening even to opinions that are contrary to one's own.
So long as we maintain dialogue, society can avoid division.
Freedom of expression is also the freedom to listen.
